title: Refund policy
content: Refunds are available within 7 days when the user provides proof of purchase. The assistant should answer refund questions concisely and cite the refund policy.

title: Metrics, traces, and logs workflow
content: Metrics detect that an incident is happening, traces localize the slow or failing span, and logs explain the root cause. A production debugging workflow should use all three signals together.

title: PII-safe logging policy
content: Do not expose PII or sensitive values in logs. Emails, phone numbers, credit card numbers, and user identifiers should be redacted, hashed, or summarized before logging.

title: Alert design
content: Alerts should be tied to user-impacting SLOs, include severity, use clear thresholds, and avoid noisy low-value signals. Good alerts include runbook context and ownership.

title: Tail latency debugging
content: Tail latency should be debugged by checking p95 and p99 metrics, then using traces to find the slow span. Logs should confirm whether the root cause is retrieval, tool failure, model latency, or downstream dependencies.

title: RAG slow incident
content: The rag_slow incident represents a retrieval latency spike. Students should identify the slow RAG span in the trace and explain how it affects end-to-end latency.

title: Tool failure incident
content: The tool_fail incident represents a vector store or tool error. Students should identify the error type from logs and connect it to failed retrieval or incomplete answers.

title: Cost spike incident
content: The cost_spike incident represents a token usage spike. Students should inspect input and output token usage and explain why output token cost increased.

title: Langfuse trace fields
content: A useful Langfuse trace includes user_id or hashed user_id, session_id, feature tag, model, latency, input and output token usage, cost, retrieval span metadata, and sanitized previews.

title: Observability dashboard panels
content: A production dashboard should show request volume, latency percentiles, error rate, token usage, cost, quality score, and recent incidents so teams can spot regressions quickly.

title: Evaluation data quality
content: Golden evaluation datasets should include expert-reviewed expected answers, difficulty levels, source document IDs, happy path cases, edge cases, adversarial prompts, and multilingual or ambiguous examples.

title: Out-of-scope handling
content: If a question is outside the assistant's domain or asks the assistant to ignore instructions, the assistant should refuse briefly and redirect to supported observability, logging, incident, or policy topics.
